content:
# User-provided Liquid Glass form/modal screenshots - Overview

Page type: form
Use cases: create project modal, workspace setup, invite member, premium onboarding form
Evidence: screenshot_observed / high
Source: local-chat-reference:2026-04-25/liquid-glass-form-modal

Visual traits:
- frosted modal panel
- soft grid background
- glass input fields
- validation glow
- large rounded submit button
- misty depth

Use when:
- create project modal
- workspace setup
- invite member
- premium onboarding form

Avoid when:
- very long form
- government form
- banking KYC
- data-heavy admin edit page

AI summary: Use Liquid Glass form/modal patterns for premium setup flows: a frosted modal card, simple stacked fields, glass inputs, colored validation glow, toggles/checkboxes, and a large luminous CTA.

# User-provided Liquid Glass form/modal screenshots - Layout Rules

Page type: form
Use cases: create project modal, workspace setup, invite member, premium onboarding form
Evidence: screenshot_observed / high
Source: local-chat-reference:2026-04-25/liquid-glass-form-modal

- Put the main form inside a single translucent frosted panel with a broad radius and subtle rim highlight.
- Use stacked labels and inputs; glass effects already add visual complexity, so form layout should stay simple.
- Show validation with colored glow plus text, for example red rim for missing category and green rim for confirmed selection.
- Use a large bottom-aligned glass CTA with soft blue/purple light for the primary action.
- Keep the background quiet: faint grid, soft gradient, or blurred side panels.

AI summary: Use Liquid Glass form/modal patterns for premium setup flows: a frosted modal card, simple stacked fields, glass inputs, colored validation glow, toggles/checkboxes, and a large luminous CTA.

AI summary: Use Liquid Glass form/modal patterns for premium setup flows: a frosted modal card, simple stacked fields, glass inputs, colored validation glow, toggles/checkboxes, and a large luminous CTA.

AI summary: Use Liquid Glass form/modal patterns for premium setup flows: a frosted modal card, simple stacked fields, glass inputs, colored validation glow, toggles/checkboxes, and a large luminous CTA.

# User-provided Liquid Glass form/modal screenshots - Accessibility Notes

Page type: form
Use cases: create project modal, workspace setup, invite member, premium onboarding form
Evidence: screenshot_observed / high
Source: local-chat-reference:2026-04-25/liquid-glass-form-modal

- Validation message must be text, not only red glow.
- Glass fields need a visible border and sufficient label contrast.
- Avoid placing important labels over noisy blurred content.

AI summary: Use Liquid Glass form/modal patterns for premium setup flows: a frosted modal card, simple stacked fields, glass inputs, colored validation glow, toggles/checkboxes, and a large luminous CTA.

# User-provided Liquid Glass form/modal screenshots - Implementation Hints

Page type: form
Use cases: create project modal, workspace setup, invite member, premium onboarding form
Evidence: screenshot_observed / high
Source: local-chat-reference:2026-04-25/liquid-glass-form-modal

- Use one glass-card wrapper for the modal and lighter glass inset surfaces for fields.
- For error fields, combine rgba red border, red outer glow, and plain text message.
- For success fields, combine green edge glow with check icon or success text.
- Use backdrop-filter only where supported and provide a translucent fallback background.

AI summary: Use Liquid Glass form/modal patterns for premium setup flows: a frosted modal card, simple stacked fields, glass inputs, colored validation glow, toggles/checkboxes, and a large luminous CTA.
